info.title: Tms Service
info.description:
Transport Management System (TMS) - Last-mile delivery orchestration and optimization platform.

Coordinates the complete delivery process from route planning to final execution, managing drivers, vehicles, and real-time operations for efficient logistics.


 **Last update**: 19 feb 26, 09:56 a. m.

tags.name: Delivery Man
tags.description:
Delivery personnel management for last-mile operations.

### Purpose
Manage delivery personnel who execute final customer deliveries on assigned routes, ensuring efficient workforce coordination and delivery quality control.

### Key Features
- **Personnel Management**: Complete delivery staff profiles with personal details and credentials
- **Route Assignments**: Coordinate delivery personnel with specific delivery routes
- **Performance Tracking**: Monitor delivery metrics, quality scores, and operational efficiency
- **Schedule Management**: Track availability and work schedules for optimal assignments
- **Training Records**: Maintain training history and certification status

### What You Can Do
- Register and manage delivery personnel profiles
- Assign delivery staff to specific routes and areas
- Track performance metrics and delivery quality
- Monitor availability and schedule assignments
- Generate workforce reports and analytics

Essential for operations managers to ensure reliable delivery execution and maintain high service quality standards.

tags.name: Driver
tags.description:
Driver profile management and administration system.

### Purpose
Manage driver profiles, credentials, and operational permissions to ensure proper access control and efficient workforce management.

### Key Features
- **Profile Management**: Complete driver registration with personal information (name, email, document number, employee ID)
- **Account Integration**: Link drivers with user accounts for system access
- **Warehouse Permissions**: Control which warehouses drivers can access for operations
- **Status Management**: Track driver status (active/inactive) and operational availability
- **Advanced Search**: Filter drivers by name, email, employee ID, and creation dates

### What You Can Do
- Register and manage driver profiles
- Set up warehouse access permissions for drivers
- Search and filter drivers by multiple criteria
- Manage driver account status and permissions
- Integrate with user management for secure access control

Essential for fleet managers and administrators to maintain proper driver credentials and ensure secure access to warehouse operations and route assignments.

tags.name: Driver Account
tags.description:
Self-service account management for drivers.

### Purpose
Allow drivers to view and manage their personal account information and warehouse assignments through a secure, self-service interface.

### Key Features
- **Profile Management**: View personal information (name, email, document number, employee ID)
- **Warehouse Selection**: Update active warehouse from authorized warehouse list
- **Account Status**: Check current account status and user profile images
- **Secure Access**: All operations restricted to authenticated driver's own account

### What You Can Do
- View your complete driver profile and account details
- Switch between authorized warehouses for work assignments
- Check account status and permissions
- Update active warehouse selection for route assignments

**IMPORTANT**: These APIs require authentication as a driver with an associated [Driver](#tag/Driver) account.

Essential for drivers to manage their work preferences and ensure proper warehouse assignments for daily operations.

tags.name: Driver Planning
tags.description:
Driver availability scheduling and workforce management system.

### Purpose
Manage driver availability schedules to optimize workforce planning and ensure proper route assignments based on driver availability.

### Availability States
Tracks three driver availability statuses:
- **Available**: Driver is ready to work and can be assigned routes
- **Not Available**: Driver is unavailable with specific reasons (vacation, sick leave, etc.)
- **Occupied**: Driver is currently assigned to active routes

### Key Features
- **Schedule Management**: Create and manage availability time slots for drivers
- **Bulk Operations**: Set availability for multiple drivers across multiple warehouses
- **Automatic Updates**: System automatically marks drivers as occupied when assigned to routes
- **Reason Tracking**: Record motives for unavailability periods
- **Warehouse Validation**: Ensure drivers can only be scheduled for authorized warehouses

### What You Can Do
- Schedule driver availability across different time periods
- Track driver status for operational planning
- Filter availability by date ranges, drivers, and warehouses
- Validate driver assignments before route creation
- Generate workforce reports and availability analytics

Essential for operations managers to ensure adequate driver coverage and optimize route assignments based on real availability.

tags.name: Route
tags.description:
Complete route execution and lifecycle management with advanced simulation capabilities.

### Purpose
Manage the entire delivery route lifecycle from creation to completion, including real-time tracking, driver coordination, and performance monitoring.

### Route Lifecycle
Routes progress through six operational states:
`created` → `scheduled` → `started` → `finished` (successful flow)
Alternative outcomes: `cancelled`, `error`

### Core Features
- **Route Execution**: Start routes with location capture, finish with performance metrics
- **Real-time Tracking**: Live updates for pickup/dropoff actions and GPS location monitoring
- **Driver Coordination**: Assignment validation, availability checking, and self-service access
- **Warehouse Integration**: Dispatch confirmation and coordination workflows
- **Automatic Scheduling**: Based on shipping time windows and operational constraints

### Advanced Route Simulation
Test and optimize routes before execution with three simulation modes:
- **Shipping-based**: Generate optimal routes from shipment lists with automatic sequencing
- **Route-based**: Simulate modifications to existing routes (created/scheduled status only)
- **Stop-based**: Custom configurations with shipment management and external optimization

### Optimization & Analysis
- **Intelligent Analysis**: Configurable parameters for warehouse constraints and stop limits
- **Capacity Validation**: Vehicle limits and delivery time window checking
- **Performance Warnings**: Alerts for capacity and time violations during simulation
- **Distance & Duration**: Automatic calculations and route metrics

### What You Can Do
- Create and manage delivery routes with automatic scheduling
- Simulate route changes before implementation
- Track route execution in real-time
- Monitor driver assignments and availability
- Analyze route performance and optimization opportunities
- Coordinate with warehouse dispatch operations

Essential for operations teams to execute efficient deliveries with real-time visibility and optimization capabilities.

tags.name: Route Dispatch
tags.description:
Warehouse dispatch coordination and package handover management system.

### Purpose
Coordinate the physical preparation and handover of packages from warehouses to drivers, ensuring smooth transition from storage to delivery execution.

### Dispatch Workflow
Manages four distinct operational states:
1. `pending` → Awaiting scheduling and preparation
2. `readyForDispatch` → All shipments scheduled and ready for pickup
3. `preparingForDispatch` → Active preparation in progress at warehouse
4. `dispatched` → Completed handover to driver with confirmation

### Key Features
- **Warehouse Coordination**: Seamless integration with warehouse operations and shipment management
- **Dispatcher Management**: Assignment tracking with ownership filtering (mine/unassigned)
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Track preparation progress and timing across all dispatch operations
- **Package Validation**: Verification and count validation during dispatch completion
- **Secure Handover**: Token-based confirmation system for route dispatch completion

### Operational Control
- **Automatic Status Transitions**: Based on shipping schedules and operational triggers
- **Performance Tracking**: Detailed timing metrics (start/end times, duration calculations)
- **Access Control**: Warehouse-specific permissions and validation
- **Audit Trail**: Comprehensive tracking of all dispatch operations and accountability

### What You Can Do
- Monitor dispatch progress across multiple warehouses
- Track dispatcher assignments and workload distribution
- Validate package counts and shipment details
- Generate dispatch confirmations and tokens
- Filter and analyze dispatch performance by various criteria

Essential for warehouse managers and dispatchers to ensure efficient package preparation and secure handover to delivery drivers.

tags.name: Route Planning
tags.description:
Strategic route optimization and planning engine for efficient delivery operations.

### Purpose
Create optimal delivery routes by analyzing shipments, vehicle capacity, driver availability, and operational constraints to maximize efficiency and minimize costs.

### Planning Process
The system follows a structured workflow:
1. **Configuration**: Set warehouse parameters, vehicle types, and delivery windows
2. **Optimization**: AI-powered analysis generates optimal route solutions
3. **Review**: Analyze proposed routes and unassigned shipments
4. **Confirmation**: Approve and create actual routes for execution

### Planning States
Track progress through eight distinct states:
- `processing` → `pendingConfirmation` → `routesCreated` (successful flow)
- Alternative outcomes: `cancelled`, `planningError`, `routeCreationFailed`, `routesPartiallyCreated`

### Key Features
- **Multi-warehouse Planning**: Handle complex scenarios across multiple locations
- **Smart Optimization**: External and internal algorithms for best route solutions
- **Constraint Management**: Vehicle capacity, time windows, shipping type restrictions
- **Unassigned Detection**: Identify and report shipments that can't be routed
- **Comprehensive Analytics**: Detailed insights on warehouses, vehicles, routes, and assignments

### What You Can Do
- Create strategic route plans with multiple parameters
- Analyze optimization results and unassigned shipments
- Modify and confirm routes before execution
- Monitor planning progress and performance metrics
- Generate bulk routes with automatic validation

Essential for operations managers to create efficient delivery strategies and optimize resource utilization across the transport network.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description:
System configuration and operational parameter management for TMS.

This module provides comprehensive management of system-wide configurations, operational parameters, business rules, user preferences, and integration settings.
It enables administrators to customize system behavior, define operational constraints, configure notifications, and manage system integrations to align with specific business requirements.

tags.name: Vehicle
tags.description:
Fleet vehicle management and real-time tracking for optimal transport operations.

### Purpose
Manage your vehicle fleet with complete registration, tracking, and operational control to ensure efficient route assignments and delivery operations.

### Key Capabilities
- **Vehicle Registration**: Complete vehicle profiles with specifications (plate, brand, model, year, capacity)
- **Real-time Tracking**: GPS location monitoring and route execution visibility
- **Fleet Management**: Status control, capacity management, and operational availability
- **Smart Integration**: Automatic vehicle-to-route matching based on capacity and constraints

### Vehicle Information
Each vehicle includes:
- Basic details (name, license plate, brand, model, year)
- Operational specs (load capacity in kg, vehicle type classification)
- Location data (GPS coordinates, tracking history)
- Status management (active, inactive, error states)

### What You Can Do
- Register and manage vehicle fleet
- Track vehicle locations in real-time
- Filter and search vehicles by multiple criteria
- Monitor vehicle utilization and performance
- Integrate with route planning for optimal assignments

Essential for route optimization systems to match vehicles with appropriate capacity and specifications to delivery requirements.

tags.name: Vehicle Type
tags.description:
Vehicle classification and capacity management for optimal fleet operations.

### Purpose
Define and manage vehicle categories with specific operational constraints to enable intelligent route planning and fleet optimization.

### Vehicle Categories
Supports five vehicle types with distinct operational profiles:
- **Bicycle**: Personal delivery for small packages
- **Motorcycle**: Quick urban deliveries
- **Car**: Standard passenger vehicle deliveries
- **Van**: Medium capacity commercial deliveries
- **Truck**: High capacity freight transport

### Key Features
- **Capacity Management**: Configure shipping limits, volume, weight, and distance constraints
- **Route Optimization**: Automatic vehicle selection based on delivery requirements
- **Fleet Classification**: Organize vehicles by type for operational planning
- **Multi-tenant Support**: Company-specific vehicle type configurations

### What You Can Do
- Create and manage vehicle type definitions
- Set operational constraints (capacity, weight, volume limits)
- Filter and search vehicle types by various criteria
- Integrate with route planning for optimal assignments

Essential for route planning systems to match the right vehicle type to delivery requirements and operational constraints.

post.summary: Assign a driver to a route

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: This refers to the `id` of the **Route** to which the driver should be assigned. See [Route](/v2/service/tms#tag/Route)

requestBody.description:
The driver assignment data.

> **IMPORTANT**: The route must be in the following statuses to assign a driver: `created`, `scheduled` or `started`.

post.description:
This API update the status of the route to `canceled`.

> The route can only be canceled if it is in status `created`, `scheduled` or `started`.

requestBody.description: The data to cancel a route

post.description:
This API is used to confirm the dispatch of a route.

> Only the driver assigned to the route has permission to update the route tracking.

requestBody.description: The data to confirm the route dispatch

post.summary: Generate Delivery Sheet from a Route
post.description:
This API generates a delivery sheet for the route

This process is available for those routes that have a  [Company](/v2/service/tms#tag/Company) related to the `useDeliveryNote` property set.

With the information of the route and its related entities it will try to generate a [Document](/v2/service/document-generator#tag/Document)
through the Document Generator Service, using the [Template](/v2/service/document-generator#tag/TemplateDocument) with the `route-delivery-sheet` template code.

In case of being able to generate it successfully in the route, the identifier of this record will be saved in the property 'deliverySheetId'.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: This refers to the `id` of the **Route**. See [Route](/v2/service/tms#tag/Route)

get.summary: Get details ​​of a route

200.description: get the details of a route

post.description:
This API update the status of the route to `finished`.

> The route need to be in status `started` to update to finish

This API allows operations team to manually finish routes and specify the status of each shipping.

**Use Cases:**
- Technical problems with mobile APP
- External deliveries made outside the normal flow
- Operational emergencies requiring route closure
- Driver incidents preventing normal completion

**Key Features:**
- Specify individual status for each shipping in the route
- Automatic route finalization when all actions are complete
- Complete traceability of manual actions

> **Important:**
- All shipments pending completion must be sent with their respective status to be updated.
- The shipments that have already been completed cannot be updated, and are not required in the request.
- The route can be in `scheduled`, or `started` status.

requestBody.description: The data to manually finish the route

post.summary: Postpone a stop on a route.
post.description:
This API is used to postpone a stop on a route.

> The route must be in the 'started' status to postpone a stop.

> **Important** The stop cannot be located in the last position.
> **Important** The stop cannot be of finish type.
> **Important** The next stop cannot be a finish action.

> If the stop is successfully postponed, it shall be placed in the last position or in the position before the finish type stop.

requestBody.description: The id of the stop to postpone

post.description:
This API update the status of the route to `started`.

> The route need to be in status `scheduled` to update to start.

requestBody.description: The data to start a route

post.description:
This API is used to update the route tracking once it has been started, i.e. the status is equal to `started`.

> Only the driver assigned to the route has permission to update the route tracking.

requestBody.description: The data to update the route tracking

patch.description: Vehicle tracking can only be updated when the route is in the `started` status.

requestBody.description: Coordinates for updating the vehicle route

get.summary: Get a route dispatch by identifier
Get a list of route dispatch
Get details ​​of a route dispatch

200.description: get the details of a route dispatch

post.description:
This API finish the dispatch of the route

> The dispatch of the route need to be in status `preparingForDispatch` to update to `dispatched`.

requestBody.description: The data to finish the dispatch of route

200.description: Response success of finishing the dispatch of the route

post.description:
This API start the dispatch of the route

> The dispatch of the route need to be in status `readyForDispatch` to update to `preparingForDispatch`.

get.summary: Get a route planning by identifier

This API is used to update the routes of a route planning

**IMPORTANT**:
- The planning status should be pending confirmation.
- One of the fields `routes` or `routingConfirmed` must be sent.
- When the planning is confirmed, the routes are then created

requestBody.description: The data to update the routes or to confirm planning

DriverAvailableFilters.name: filters
DriverAvailableFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

RouteFilters.name: filters
RouteFilters.description:
The filters to apply to the list.

**Default Date Filtering**: If no `id` filter is provided and no date filters (`dateCreated`, `dateModified`) are specified, the API automatically applies a default date filter for the last month (`dateCreated`). This ensures that by default, only recent routes are returned.

ownership.description:
Filters routes by driver ownership. Only available for logged users with an active driver account.

- `onlyMine`: Returns only routes assigned to the current driver with status `created`, `scheduled` or `started`
- `mineAndUnassigned`: Returns routes either assigned to the current driver or unassigned with status `scheduled` or `started`

>If status filter is also provided, it must match the allowed statuses for each ownership type.

dateCreated.description: A range for creation day. If provided, this will override the default date filtering behavior.

dateModified.description: A range for last modification day. If provided, this will override the default date filtering behavior.

RouteDispatchPackageCount.name: packageCount
RouteDispatchPackageCount.description: Whether to include the package count in the response

RouteDispatchFilters.name: filters
RouteDispatchFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

ownership.description: Filters by dispatcher assigned and/or unassigned routes. Available only for logged users.

dateModified.description: The date of the last modification

RoutePlanningShippingFilters.name: filters
RoutePlanningShippingFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

shippingAssignmentStatus.description:
Filters shipments based on their assignment status in route planning.

- `assigned`: Returns shipments that have been assigned to a route.
- `unassigned`: Returns shipments that have not been assigned to any route.

x-janis-total.description: The total amount of records in the list

x-janis-total-created.description: The total amount of records created

x-janis-total-updated.description: The total amount of records updated

schema.description: Object with information on driver plannings created, updated, not modified and with errors

itemsUpdated.description: Driver plannings that were updated succesfully

itemsNotModified.description: Driver plannings that did not need to be modified

itemsError.description: Driver plannings that could not be processed because of an error

RouteCapacitySchemaBatchResponse.description: Response object for batch creation or update of route capacity schemas. Includes lists of created, updated, unchanged, and errored schemas.

itemsCreated.description: List of route capacity schemas successfully created

itemsUpdated.description: List of route capacity schemas successfully updated

itemsNotModified.description: List of route capacity schemas that were not modified (no changes detected)

itemsError.description: List of route capacity schemas that encountered errors during processing

id.description: The unique identifier of the item

displayId.description:
It is a unique reference ID that is generated and assigned automatically when the record is created, it cannot be modified and it is structured in three parts:

- **Date**: It is the date in the format `YYMMDD`.
- **Delimiter**: It is the character `-`.
- **String random**: It is a group of six random alphanumeric characters.

Example: `210412-MI92CT`

shippingId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Shipping](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Shipping) in Delivery Service

warehouseId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse) in WMS Service

deliverySheetId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Document](/v2/service/document-generator#tag/Document) in Document Generator Service

dateModified.description: The date of the last modification

userCreated.description: The ID of the user who created the item

userModified.description: The ID of the user who last modified the item

FunctionTotals.description: The totals data. It is only available when `calculateTotals` parameter is active

total.description: The total items found in database applied filters

pages.description: The total pages for the filters applied

itemsQuantity.description: The quantity of products in the [Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the payment (including interests) in [Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

paymentMethod.description: The name of a [Payment Method](/v2/service/jef#tag/Payment-Method) in JEF Service

isPaymentUponDelivery.description: Indicates if the payment must be validated upon delivery in [Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

isOnlinePaymentUponDelivery.description: Indicates if the online payment must be validated upon delivery in [Order] (/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

currency.description: The ISO3 Code of the currency in [Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

productGroupIds.description: The Product Groups IDs. This refer to the `id` of the **Product Group** Entity. See [Product Group](/v2/service/delivery#tag/ProductGroup).

length.description: The length of the second factor value. If it is a phrase, the words will be added

value.description: The value of second factor. This field can only be seen by users who have the `delivery:show-second-factor` permission, configured in [ID Profile](/v2/service/id#tag/Profile)

deliveryOptions.description: It contains the settings of the delivery options of the shipping. See [Shipping](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Shipping)

requirePhotoOnDelivery.description: Indicates if the shipping requires a photo on delivery

fullname.description:
The name of the company.

isFinished.description:
Indicates whether the event has concluded.

**Note**: This field is applicable only for events where `availability` is `occupied`.
It is set to `true` once the route started by the driver and associated with this event has ended.

timeSlots.description: The time slots of the planning

DriverPlanningBatchBase.description: Object with the basic information of a driver's planning to be created or updated

dateStart.description:
The date that the planning starts

**Important** 
- The date must be greater than the current date
- Required if the `dateEnd` field is sent

dateEnd.description:
The date that the planning ends

**Important** 
- The date must be greater than the `dateStart` field
- Required if the `dateStart` field is sent

availability.description: The availability status of the planning

motive.description:
The name of the motive

**Important** 
- Required if the `availability` field is `notAvailable`
- The motive must belong to the Tms service and the target must be `tms:driver-availability:not-available`

comment.description:
The comment of the planning

**Important** 
- Required if the `availability` field is `notAvailable`

DriverPlanningBatchResponse.description: Object with basic information on the response of the processing of drivers plannings

errorMessage.example: Failed to save the driver planning

Object representing the required data to create a new route capacity schema.
    
Including reference identifiers, name, status, and detailed capacity configuration per warehouse, time window, and vehicle type

Object representing the required data to update an existing route capacity schema.
    
Including reference identifiers, name, status, and detailed capacity configuration per warehouse, time window, and vehicle type

calculateDuration.description: Determines whether to calculate the duration of the route

displayId.description:
Display identifier of the simulated route. If the simulation is based on an existing route (`routeId` provided),
it returns the route's current `displayId`. Otherwise, a new `displayId` is generated for the simulation.

value.description: Boolean value indicating whether the route can be optimized

reason.description: Reason why a route cannot be optimized in this case
reason.example: The route must have only one warehouse

warnings.description: List of warnings generated during the simulation.

The number of shipments assigned to the route exceeds the maximum shipping capacity supported by the assigned vehicle

end.description: The end of the shipping schedule

itemsQuantity.description: The quantity of products in the [OMS Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the payment (including interests) in [OMS Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

paymentMethod.description: The name of a [Payment Method](/v2/service/jef#tag/Payment-Method) in JEF Service

isPaymentUponDelivery.description: Indicates if the payment must be validated upon delivery in [OMS Order](/v2/service/oms#tag/Orders)

id.description: The `id` of the related shipping. See [Shipping](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Shipping)

destinationId.description: The destination id to set as destination point

IsDestinationOptimizableSimulate.description:
Indicates whether the stop can be considered as an optimizable destination during route optimization simulations.

A stop will have `isDestinationOptimizable` set to `true` if:
- The stop's kind is 'warehouse'.
- The stop's kind is `customer` and there are no shipments with reverse logistics associated with it.

> This property is available when `canBeOptimized.value` is set to `true`.

VehicleConfiguration.description: Configuration parameters that define the shipping capabilities and constraints of a vehicle.

maxShippingQuantity.description:
Maximum number of shipments the vehicle can carry simultaneously.

This limit is used to validate and control the assignment of shipments to the vehicle.

startTime.description: The start time of the window in ISO 8601 format

endTime.description: The end time of the window in ISO 8601 format

Warning.description: Object containing detailed information about a routing warning.

code.description: Unique code identifying the type of warning.

message.description: Human-readable description providing more details about the warning.
message.example: Some shipments fall outside the route\'s delivery time window

OptimizeRoute.title: Optimize route
OptimizeRoute.description:
Request to optimize the current route and append a final destination point.

The `optimizeRoute` flag must be explicitly set to `true`.

optimizeRoute.description: Must be set to `true` to trigger route optimization

AddDestinationPoint.title: Add destination point
AddDestinationPoint.description:
Request to add a final destination point without optimizing the route.

The `optimizeRoute` flag must be explicitly set to `false`.

optimizeRoute.description: Must be set to `false`. Indicates that route optimization is not required.

RemoveDestinationPoint.title: Delete destination point
RemoveDestinationPoint.description:
Request to remove the current final destination point from the route.

The `optimizeRoute` flag must be explicitly set to `false`.

maxProductQuantity.description: The vehicle type's max products quantity

fuelConsumption.description: Indicates the consumption in liters per kilometer traveled of the type of vehicle

userId.description: The user identifier. This must be the `id` of a [User](/v2/service/id#tag/User)

items.description: A warehouse identifier. This must be the `id` of a [WMS Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse)

items.description: A warehouse identifier. This must be the `id` of a [WMS Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse)

Route.description: A route from a list of routes

start.description: The beggining of the route schedule

end.description: The end of the route schedule

companyId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Company](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Company) in Delivery Service

items.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Carrier](/v2/service/wms#tag/Carrier) in Delivery Service

driverId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Driver](#tag/Driver)

deliveryManId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Delivery Man](#tag/Delivery-Man)

vehicleId.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Vehicle](#tag/Vehicle)

shippingQuantity.description: The amount of shippings included in the route

stopsQuantity.description: The amount of stops included in the route

ordersQuantity.description: The amount of orders included in the route

autoSchedule.description: Determines whether the `schedule` field is calculated based on route shippings

x-enumDescriptions.created: Initial state indicating that the route has been successfully created in the system
x-enumDescriptions.scheduled: The route is automatically set to this status when all associated shippings are in scheduled status
x-enumDescriptions.started: The driver has begun the route execution and is actively performing deliveries/pickups according to the planned stops
x-enumDescriptions.finished: All planned stops and actions in the route have been completed successfully by the driver
x-enumDescriptions.cancelled: The route has been terminated before completion due to operational issues, weather conditions, or other external factors
x-enumDescriptions.error: The route encountered a critical system or operational error that prevented normal execution or completion

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the route is completed. A route is considered completed when it has `started` status and all actions in all stops have a final status (`completed` or `failed`), or when it has `finished` status.

dateStarted.description: Date when the driver started the route

dateFinished.description: Date when the driver finished the route

scheduled.description: Represents the scheduled time in minutes that it will take the driver to complete the route

expected.description: Represents the expected time in minutes that it will take the driver to complete the route

actual.description: Represents the time in minutes it took the driver to complete the route

discrepancy.description:
Discrepancy of the route represent the difference between the expected and the actual duration in minutes
Only available when the route is finished

result.description: The result data of the route. This info is calculate when finish the route.

delayToStart.description:
Represents the delay time in minutes to start the route..

> It is calculated by subtracting the `dateStarted` and `schedule.start` fields.
> If the value is negative it means that the route was started on time.

delayToFinished.description:
Represents the delay time in minutes to finish the route.

> It is calculated by subtracting the `dateFinished` and `schedule.end` fields.
> If the value is negative it means that the route was completed on time.

dropoffDeliveredOnTime.description: Quantity of dropoff delivered on time

dropoffDeliveredOutOfTime.description: Quantity of dropoff delivered out of time

routeTakenCoordinates.description: The route taken parsed in coordinates

expected.description:
It represents the distance in kilometers that the driver is expected to travel to complete the route

> The value is calculated when the route is scheduled or when there are changes in shippings.

origin.description: The coordinates of the route start

lat.description: The latitude of the coordinates of the route start

lng.description: The longitude of the coordinates of the route start

routePlanningId.description:
This refer to the `id` of the [Route Planning](#tag/Route-Planning).

**Note:**
This field exists when the route is created by a route planning.

vehicleTypeId.description:
This refer to the `id` of the [Vehicle Type](#tag/Vehicle-Type)

**Note:**
This field exists when the route is created by a route planning.

orderIds.description: List of unique order identifiers from all shippings in the route

order.description:
Detailed information about the order. This property will only appear when the route has a single order.
If the route has multiple orders or no orders, this information will not be included.

deliveryConditions.description: Special conditions that apply to the route based on its orders

hasOnlinePaymentUponDelivery.description: Indicates if at least one order in the route has online payment upon delivery

hasPaymentUponDelivery.description: Indicates if at least one order in the route has payment upon delivery

shippingDeliveredOutOfTime.description: Quantity of shippings delivered out of time

routeAlert.description: Field that shows the possible alerts in a route

x-enumDescriptions.outOfTime:
Applied to routes in final status (finished) that completed with positive discrepancy, 
meaning the actual duration exceeded the expected duration.
x-enumDescriptions.delayed:
Applied when: (1) current time has passed the scheduled end time and the route is in 'started' status, or 
(2) current time has passed the scheduled start time but the route has not yet started.
x-enumDescriptions.expired:
Applied when the current time has passed the scheduled end time and the route was never started, 
indicating the route missed its entire execution window.

etaThresholds.description:
Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) threshold timestamps that represent different time reference points for route monitoring.
These timestamp values are calculated based on percentages of the route's expected duration.

warning.description:
Warning threshold timestamp: represents the date and time when the route reaches 55% of its expected duration.
This timestamp serves as a reference point to evaluate if the route is progressing within expected timeframes.

critical.description:
Critical threshold timestamp: represents the date and time when the route reaches 80% of its expected duration.
This timestamp serves as a reference point to assess potential delays in route completion.

exceeded.description:
Exceeded threshold timestamp: represents the date and time when the route reaches 100% of its expected duration.
This timestamp serves as a reference point to determine if the route has exceeded its planned completion time.

eta.description: Estimated time of arrival of the driver at the stop

time.description: Estimated time of arrival expressed in a single value

window.description: Estimated time of arrival expressed in range

isOnTime.description:
Indicate whether the actions performed at the stop will be executed at the scheduled time.

> This field exists if the stop requires evaluating the time of the actions.

**Important:**
- If the stop is a warehouse, it is assessed that actions related to deliveries will be executed at the scheduled time.
- If the stop is a customer, all the actions are evaluated to determine if they will be executed at the scheduled time.

relatedShippingId.description: This refers to the `id` of the related shipping that has the customer's pickup address

autoFail.description: Indicates if the action was automatically marked as failed.

deliveredOnTime.description: Indicates if the action was delivered on time

RouteStopActionStatus.description: Represents the current status of a route stop action, indicating the progress of the driver's task at a specific location.

x-enumDescriptions.pending: It is the initial state of the action
x-enumDescriptions.onTheWay: It is the state that indicates that the driver is on the way to the address
x-enumDescriptions.arrived: It is the status that indicates that the driver has already arrived at the address
x-enumDescriptions.completed: It is the state that indicates that the driver completed the action
x-enumDescriptions.failed: It is the state that indicates that the driver could not complete the action
x-enumDescriptions.postponed: It is the state that indicates that the driver postponed the action

comment: Se reasignó el conductor debido a un cambio en la jornada laboral
Se detectó un error en la asignación y se corrigió la ruta del conductor

example.address: Avenida Santa Fe, 3253, Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina, C1414AAA
Avenida Santa Fe, 3253, Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina, C1414AAA

newDriverId.description: The new driver identifier. This must be the `id` of a [Driver](/v2/service/tms#tag/Driver)

comment.description: Additional comments about the change driver
example: The driver was changed to John Doe

autoFail.description: Indicates if the action was automatically marked as failed.
Indicates if the dropoff failed automatically

deliveredOnTime.description: Indicates if the dropoff was delivered on time

address.example: Av. Corrientes 1234, CABA, Buenos Aires, Argentina, C1043AAZ

RouteTrackingAction.description: The action of the tracking event

RouteTrackingUserId.description: The user identifier. This must be the `id` of a [User](/v2/service/id#tag/User)

RouteTrackingMotiveId.description: The motive identifier. This must be the `id` of a [Motive](/v2/service/management#tag/Motive)

RouteListItem.description: A route item from the list endpoint (excludes tracking, stops and routeTaken properties)

firstStop.description: The first stop of the route with normalized fields

x-enumDescriptions.customer: Indicates that the first stop corresponds to a customer location
x-enumDescriptions.warehouse: Indicates that the first stop corresponds to a warehouse location

displayId.description:
Display identifier for the route.

If not provided, a `displayId` will be automatically generated with format `YYMMDD-{randomId}`.

If provided but doesn't have the prefix format (`YYMMDD-`), the prefix will be automatically added using the current date.
The prefix format is 6 digits (YYMMDD) followed by a dash. For example, if you provide `MYROUTE123`, it will be converted to `220530-MYROUTE123` (using current date).

If the `displayId` already has a valid prefix format, it will be kept unchanged.

Display identifier for the route.

If provided but doesn't have the prefix format (`YYMMDD-`), the prefix will be automatically added using the route's creation date (`dateCreated`).
The prefix format is 6 digits (YYMMDD) followed by a dash. For example, if you provide `MYROUTE123` and the route was created on `2024-01-16`, it will be converted to `240116-MYROUTE123`.

If the `displayId` already has a valid prefix format, it will be kept unchanged.

This refers to the `id` of the **Motive** for the driver change. See [Motive](/v2/service/management#tag/Motive)

RouteVehicleTrackingBatch.title: Batch
RouteVehicleTrackingBatch.description: A list of route status coordinates

order.description:
The order information associated with the route dispatch.
> This property is only present when the dispatch has exactly one order.
> If the dispatch has multiple orders or no orders, this property will not be present.

orderIds.description:
The list of order IDs associated with the route dispatch.
> This property contains all unique order IDs from the shippings assigned to the route dispatch.

dispatchDate.description:
The date of the route dispatch.
> This date is calculated based on the lowest dispatch date of the shippings assigned to the route dispatch

shippingIds.description: The shippings assigned to the route dispatch

shippings.description: A key-value object containing the shippings assigned to the route dispatch

additionalProperties.description: A unique identifier for the shipping. This must be an `id` of an shipping from the route.

dispatchDate.description: The dispatch date of the shipping

isScheduled.description: Indicates if the shipping is scheduled

pickupStatus.description: The current status of the pickup action. This indicates the progress of the driver's task when collecting the shipping.

orders.description: The orders associated with the shipping

totals.description: The totals of the route dispatch

shippingCount.description: The total of shippings assigned to the route dispatch

ordersCount.description: The total of unique orders assigned to the route dispatch

packageCount.description: The total of packages assigned to the route dispatch (Only present when send parameter `packageCount` is true)

dispatchStartTime.description: The start time of the route dispatch

dispatchEndTime.description: The end time of the route dispatch

dispatchDuration.description: The duration of the route dispatch in minutes

dispatchToken.description: The token of the route dispatch in numeric format with 6 characters

signature.description: The signature of the dispatch of the route

status.description: The status of the route dispatch

x-enumDescriptions.pending: The route dispatch is waiting to be processed and assigned to a dispatcher
x-enumDescriptions.readyForDispatch: The route dispatch has been processed and is ready to be picked up by the driver
x-enumDescriptions.preparingForDispatch: The warehouse staff is currently preparing and gathering the items for dispatch
x-enumDescriptions.dispatched: The route dispatch has been successfully picked up by the driver and is in transit
x-enumDescriptions.notDispatched: The route dispatch could not be completed due to issues during the pickup process

RouteDispatchDetail.description: The details of a route dispatch

route.description: The route related to the dispatch

driver.description: The driver related to the route

warehouse.description: The warehouse related to the dispatch

packages.description: The packages grouped by shipping. See [Package](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Package)

RouteDispatchFinish.description: The data to finish the dispatch of route

packageIds.description: All package ids of the linked shippings of the dispatch route

RoutePlanning.description: Object with the information of a route planning

schedule.description: The schedule of the route planning

from.description: The beggining of the route planning schedule

to.description: The end of the route planning schedule

status.description:
The status of the route planning
- **processing** The initial state of route planning.
- **pendingConfirmation** When route planning is processed and awaiting confirmation from the user.
- **cancelled** The status when the user cancels route planning.
- **planningError** The status when some route planning process fails.
- **creatingRoutes** This status indicates that the planned routes are being created.
- **routesCreated** This status indicates that the planned routes have been created.
- **routesPartiallyCreated** This status indicates that the planned routes have been partially created.
- **routeCreationFailed** This status indicates that the planned routes have failed to be created.

warehouseIds.description: The list of warehouses included in the planning

shippingIds.description: The list of shippings included in the planning

totals.description: Summary totals for the route planning process

warehouseCount.description: The number of warehouses included in the planning

vehicleTypeCount.description: The number of vehicle types considered in the planning

shippingCount.description: The total number of shipments included in the planning

assignedShippingCount.description: The number of shipments assigned to a route

unassignedShippingCount.description: The number of shipments not assigned to any route

onlyShippingsReadyForPickup.description:
Determines if the planning should consider only shippings ready for pickup

**Note**
- When this field is not sent, the client setting (`route/simulation/onlyShippingsReadyForPickup`) will be used to determine if only shippings ready for pickup should be considered.

PlanningCondition.description:
Configuration for the route planning

**Important** 
- The combination of warehouse and shipping type is unique and cannot be replicated.
- If no shipping types are sent in a planning it is assumed that the warehouse has all shipping types configured.
- At least one vehicle type with a quantity greater than zero must be sent in each route planning.

shippingTypeIds.description: The list of shipping types included in the planning

vehicleConfigurations.description: The vehicle configurations used in the planning

types.description:
The vehicle types considered for planning

**Note**
- For each type of vehicle, a quantity of vehicles or an arrangement of work windows with their respective quantity of vehicles must be configured.

VehicleConfigurationWithoutWindow.description: Configuration of the vehicle types used in the planning

id.description: The unique identifier of the vehicle type. See [Vehicle Type](/v2/service/tms#tag/Vehicle-Type)

quantity.description: The number of vehicles of this type included in the planning

VehicleConfigurationWithWindow.description: Configuration of the vehicle types used in the planning

windows.description: Configuration of the quantity of vehicles by time slots

VehicleTypeWindow.description: Defines a time window configuration for a specific vehicle type to be used during planning

quantity.description: The number of vehicles of this type to be considered in the planning process

startTime.description: The start date and time of the availability window for this vehicle type in `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ` format

endTime.description: The end date and time of the availability window for this vehicle type in `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ` format

vehicleType.description: The vehicle type of the route

warehouses.description: A key-value object containing the warehouses assigned to the route.

additionalProperties.description: A unique identifier for the warehouse. This must be an `id` of an warehouse from the route.

shippings.description: A key-value object containing the shippings assigned to the route.

additionalProperties.description: A unique identifier for the shipping. This must be an `id` of an shipping from the route.

UnassignedShippings.description: The unassigned shippings of the route planning

shippings.description: A key-value object containing the shippings assigned to the route.

additionalProperties.description: A unique identifier for the shipping. This must be an `id` of an shipping from the route.

totals.description: The totals of the unassigned shippings

groupShippings.description:
Represents shippings grouped based on two possible criteria: warehouse and company.

**Grouping Criteria:**
- If a shipping has an associated company, it will be grouped by both warehouse and company.
- If a shipping does not have an associated company, it will only be grouped by warehouse.

reasons.description: The reasons of the unassigned shipping

code.description: The code of the reason provide by here

description.example: The shipping requires a skill that is not available

RoutePlanningDetails.description: The details of a route planning

routes.description: The routes of the route planning

RoutePlanningRoutesPost.description: The data to update the routes or to confirm planning

routingConfirmed.description: Determines whether the planning is confirmed to generate the routes

routes.description: Information for updating route planning routes

id.description:
Unique identifier of the planned route

**IMPORTANT**:
When the field is not sent, it is determined that a new planned route must be created.

vehicleTypeId.description:
The identifier of the vehicle type

**IMPORTANT**:
  When the id field is not sent, this field is required, to determine the vehicle type of the route.
